name: Dippy Snacks
recipeIngredient: 1 Stick (1/2 cup) butter or margarine, softened
recipeInstructions:
With carrots, celery, green pepper strips, cucumber slices, cherry tomatoes, cauliflower pieces, or crackers, try any of the following:
CHEESE SPREAD
- garlic powder
Blend shortening with cottage cheese using a blender or baby food grinder for smoother consistency. Add garlic powder to taste.
Refrigerate in a covered container.
DIP IT AGAIN
1 (8 oz) container of plain yogurt 1 cup sour cream 1 pkg onion soup mix
Combine and chill before serving.
BETTER-WITH-BUTTERMILK DIP
1 cup buttermilk 2 cups mayonnaise 1 cup sour cream or yogurt 1 envelope Ranch style dressing mix
Stir together and chill. For salad dressing use, substitute a second cup of buttermilk for the sour cream or yogurt.
Hint: Use cucumber "coins" instead of crackers.
OTHER QUICK DIPPIES that make up in small quantities:
- Blend 1/3 to 1/2 of a mashed banana with 2 or 3 tbsp mayonnaise.
- Whipe together 1 tsp smooth peanut butter with 1 tsp mayonnaise. If still too thick, add a few drops of milk.
- Mix 1/2 tsp lemon juice and 1/2 tsp honey or sugar to 1 or 2 tsp mayonnaise.
CUTTING UP WITH KIDS
Have your children help you cut up green onions, parsley, lettuce, cheese slices, fruit, etc., using scissors instead of a knife. Using scissors helps coordination (YES!) and keeps them busy for a long time!
CHERRY BALLS
Stuff whole (or half) cherry tomatoes with cream cheese, a cheese spread, egg salad, or tuna salad. Thinking of snacks as hors d'oeuvres rather than desserts will open up new ways to enjoy this category.
